title: SpecManager – a full agile team for founders, as a Claude Code plugin
description: Spec-driven development for Claude Code. Turns AI coding into a gated PRD → Architecture → Plan → Build pipeline on a local kanban board, backed by git-tracked markdown.

title: OctoPerf MCP – drive load tests from any LLM (OAuth 2.1, no API key)
description: MCP server for OctoPerf load testing. Drive performance tests from any AI agent (Claude.ai, Claude Code, Cursor) through the Model Context Protocol with OAuth 2.1 authentication.

title: Claude Code plugin to draw feedback and send it back into the session
description: Claude Code plugin: draw on a live frontend in the Playwright browser, annotated screenshot flows back into the session. Local, no cloud — keeps annotations in-context for the coding agent.

title: Closedtab: a shared record for human-agent teams
description: Shared record/documentation system for human-agent teams — a lightweight npm package that maintains context and task history between human developers and AI coding agents across sessions.

title: Create sandboxed rich-text telegram agents with a single config file
description: Single-config-file framework for creating sandboxed rich-text Telegram agents. Deploy autonomous agents on Telegram with isolated environments and rich message formatting.

title: Using Local Coding Agents
description: Sebastian Raschka walks through running coding agents entirely on local hardware — covering local LLM setup, tool configurations, agent orchestration without cloud APIs, and practical benchmarks of local vs hosted coding agent performance.

title: Context engineering for analytics agents: six months of building and rebuilding
description: Lessons from six months of building analytics agents — how to structure context, what worked, what failed, and practical patterns for keeping agents focused on the right data.

title: TesterArmy (YC P26) – Agents that test web and mobile apps
description: YC-backed platform using AI agents to test web and mobile applications, catching bugs before users do. Automated testing with natural language test specifications.

title: 2026 GPU Price Report — cast.ai
description: Comprehensive GPU pricing report covering cloud GPU costs across providers (AWS, GCP, Azure), spot vs on-demand pricing, and trends affecting AI inference and training costs in 2026.

title: What happened after 2,000 people tried to hack my AI assistant
description: Simon Willison reports on an LLM prompt injection challenge where 2,000 participants tested his AI assistant. Detailed breakdown of attack patterns, defense failures, and lessons for building agent systems that interact with untrusted content.

title: Incident Report: CVE-2026-LGTM
description: Postmortem on a prompt injection vulnerability in an AI-powered code review tool. Covers the attack chain, impact scope, and mitigations that every AI coding agent should implement.

title: Coding agents in the social sciences
description: Anthropic surveys 1,260 social scientists on AI and coding agent adoption in academic research, revealing how coding agents are being used for data analysis, literature review, and research workflows beyond computer science.

title: Mapping AI-enabled cyber threats (Attack Navigator)
description: Anthropic maps real-world AI-enabled cyber attacks onto the MITRE ATT&CK framework, analyzing how threat actors weaponize AI for cyber operations with detailed techniques and mitigations.

title: Measuring LLMs' impact on N-day exploits
description: Anthropic evaluates how much LLMs accelerate N-day exploit development — vulnerabilities that are publicly disclosed but only partially patched. Quantifies the risk LLMs pose to the patching window.

title: Project Fetch: Phase two
description: Anthropic tests whether Claude can help perform sophisticated robotics tasks. Claude Opus 4.7, operating without human assistance, was about 20 times faster than the fastest human team at completed tasks.

title: Agent-Native Immune System: Architecture, Taxonomy, and Engineering
description: Proposes an agent-native immune system architecture for autonomous agents with persistent memory, tool-use protocols, and multi-agent collaboration — addressing the expanded AI threat landscape beyond traditional chatbot security.

title: Govern the Repository, Not the Agent: Measuring Ecosystem-Level Risk in AI-Native Software
description: Argues that as autonomous coding agents open and merge PRs at scale, evaluation should shift from individual agent behavior to ecosystem-level risks in shared repositories.

title: ToolPrivacyBench: Benchmarking Purpose-Bound Privacy in Tool-Using LLM Agents
description: Benchmark evaluating whether tool-using LLM agents respect purpose-bound privacy constraints when invoking external tools and executing multi-step tasks, revealing significant gaps in current systems.

title: ATOD: Annealed Turn-aware On-policy Distillation for Multi-turn Autonomous Agents
description: Training method for small language-model agents on long-horizon interactive tasks, combining fast imitation with reward-driven improvement via on-policy distillation with teacher guidance.

title: Grounded Iterative Language Planning: How Parameterized World Models Reduce Hallucination Propagation in LLM Agents
description: Shows that agent-based world models (LLM-calling) hallucinate state changes, while parameterized world models (simulators) anchor planning in accurate state tracking — reducing hallucination propagation in multi-step agent tasks.

title: Agentic Hardware Design as Repository-Level Code Evolution
description: Presents HORIZON, a self-evolving agent framework treating hardware design as repository-level code evolution. A Markdown harness is compiled into a project pack with domain knowledge and executable generators.

title: MCP Office Hours: Protocol Design, Servers, and OAuth Adoption — June 2026 Update
description: Community roundup from MCP office hours covering growing OAuth adoption across MCP servers, new transport patterns, and progress toward a 1.1 transport compatibility target.

title: When Does Combining Language Models Help? A Co-Failure Ceiling on Routing, Voting, and Mixture-of-Agents Across 67 Frontier Models
description: Shows that multi-model systems (routing, voting, mixture-of-agents) have a co-failure ceiling: when models share failure modes, combining them provides diminishing returns. Empirical analysis across 67 frontier models.

title: AI and Liability
description: Simon Willison explores the evolving legal landscape of AI liability — who is responsible when AI agents make mistakes, and how the law is catching up to autonomous system behavior.

title: Kog Laneformer 2B: The Latency-First Model Behind Kog Inference Engine
description: Deep dive into Kog's latency-first 2B parameter model designed for their inference engine. Covers architecture choices, optimization techniques, and benchmark results prioritizing response time over raw throughput.

title: GPT-5.5 Instant (June 2026) Intelligence, Performance and Price Analysis
description: Comprehensive benchmark analysis of OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Instant model — quality scores, price per token, tokens per second, time to first token, and comparison across the model landscape.

title: Introducing Modal Auto Endpoints: Optimized inference you actually own
description: Modal's new Auto Endpoints feature provides optimized serverless inference infrastructure — automatically scaling, with user-owned infrastructure, designed for AI application deployment.

title: Vibe Coding to Agentic Engineering with Claude Code
description: A repeatable three-phase Claude Code workflow: structured prompt, plan mode, and review. Get reliable code from your coding agent every session.

title: Your App Should Ship an MCP Server
description: I embedded an MCP server inside a native Rust prose editor. It became the single most impactful architectural decision I've made, not for users, but for how I build the product itself.

title: OpenAI boardmember Zico Kolter and Gray Swan CEO Matt Fredrikson join swyx to explain why AI security is not just “cybersecurity with AI”
description: Deep-dive on AI safety and red-teaming with Zico Kolter and Matt Fredrikson, covering frontier model evaluation, jailbreak research, and the practical challenges of aligning powerful systems.

title: lkerriso/agentic-ai-local-to-kubernetes — GitHub (15 stars)
description: Companion repository for the talk, Agentic AI: Going from Local to Kubernetes

title: Show HN: Tecture – A navigable architecture map from your coding agent
description: HN-discussed tool that lets coding agents generate an interactive architecture map of a repository, helping developers understand system structure after autonomous refactors.

title: Beyond Function Calling: Benchmarking Tool-Using Agents under Tool-Environment Unreliability
description: arXiv paper by Yang Tian, Zhengpeng Shi, Bo Zhao. Large language models are increasingly deployed as agents that solve tasks by interacting with external tool environments. Although recent tool-use benchmarks increasingly cover complex task settings, they still largely assume clean, stable, and trustworthy tool environments, leaving tool-environmen

title: rickicode/patchright-browser — GitHub (15 stars)
description: MCP server for browser automation via Patchright. Multi-profile Chromium, headed/headless, StreamableHTTP protocol. Includes dashboard, skills, and AI agent setup.

title: agent-scan: Security Scanner for AI Agents, MCP Servers, and Agent Skills
description: Snyk's open-source security scanner for AI agents, MCP servers, and agent skills, scanning for vulnerable tool calls, unsafe permissions, and supply-chain risks in agent workflows.

title: dev-machine-guard: Scan Your Dev Machine for AI Agents and MCP Servers
description: Lightweight local scanner that inventories AI agents, MCP servers, IDE extensions, and suspicious packages on a developer machine, surfacing shadow AI tooling in seconds.

title: Show HN: AnswerJournal – An MCP server to save and share AI answers
description: An MCP server that captures and shares AI agent answers across sessions, turning ephemeral outputs into a searchable knowledge base for teams.

title: AgentRecall-MCP: Correction-First Persistent Memory for AI Agents
description: Open-source MCP server + SDK + CLI that gives coding agents correction-first persistent memory, compounding decisions, lessons, and facts across sessions with local-first storage.

title: OPFS + Pyodide test harness
description: Technical walkthrough on building a test harness using Origin Private File System and Pyodide, enabling browser-native file access for agent evaluation workflows.

title: How to run any model inside Claude Code
description: Runs GLM 5.2 inside Claude Code with the same tools, skills, and agent loop using three environment variables, demonstrating backend-agnostic model routing for coding agents.

title: NatureBench: Can Coding Agents Match the Published SOTA of Nature-Family Papers?
description: arXiv paper by Yuru Wang, Lejun Cheng, Yuxin Zuo, Sihang Zeng, Bingxiang He, Che Jiang, Junlin Yang, Yuchong Wang, Kaikai Zhao, Weifeng Huang, Kai Tian, Zhenzhao Yuan, Jincheng Zhong, Weizhi Wang, Ning Ding, Bowen Zhou, Kaiyan Zhang. We introduce NatureBench, a cross-discipline benchmark of 90 tasks distilled from peer-reviewed Nature-family publications, designed to evaluate whether AI coding agents can move beyond reproduction toward discovery on real scientific problems. NatureBench is built on NatureGym, an automated pipelin

title: Top 10 AI Stories: June 23, 2026 Daily Roundup
description: Daily roundup covering SpaceX's $60B acquisition of Cursor, ChatGPT falling below 50% market share for the first time, the ongoing Fable 5 ban entering Day 11, and the upcoming Gemini 3.5 Pro launch.

title: Forecasting Enterprise AI Spend: See the Bill Before It Lands
description: Practical guide to building forward-looking AI cost forecasts using SARIMAX and Prophet, with a focus on why attribution alone fails and how proper forecasting prevents surprise budget overruns like Uber's 4-month AI spend blowout.

title: OpenThoughts-Agent: Data Recipes for Agentic Models
description: Open-source data recipes for training broadly capable agentic models, addressing the open question of how to curate training data for agents beyond single-benchmark efforts.

title: World Models in Pieces: Structural Certification for General Agents
description: Formalizes structural certification for general agents in big-world regimes, where universal capability is impossible and guarantees must be specialized across agent world-model pieces.

title: SHERLOC: Structured Diagnostic Localization for Code Repair Agents
description: Fault-localization framework that gives code-repair agents actionable root-cause diagnoses instead of only retrieving file locations, reducing wasted budget on localization without editing direction.

title: Are We Ready For An Agent-Native Memory System?
description: Tracks the evolution of LLM agent memory from simple retrieval-augmentation into a full data-management system supporting persistent storage, consolidation, and lifecycle governance.

title: Qwen-AgentWorld: Language World Models for General Agents
description: Investigates language-model-based world models to push reasoning and planning boundaries for general agents, formalizing how text-world dynamics can serve as agent cognition.

title: MEMPROBE: Probing Long-Term Agent Memory via Hidden User-State Recovery
description: Probes whether long-term agent memory can accurately recover hidden user state across sessions, revealing discrepancies between claimed personalization and actual retention.

title: Grad Detect: Gradient-Based Hallucination Detection in LLMs
description: Hallucination detector that inspects internal gradients and activations rather than output distributions alone, flagging false claims before they reach downstream applications.

title: CrossPool: Efficient Multi-LLM Serving for Cold MoE Models through KV-Cache and Weight Disaggregation
description: Disaggregates KV-cache and model weights to serve cold mixture-of-experts models efficiently, reducing GPU memory waste when sparse requests hit uncommon expert shards.

title: Privacy-Preserving RAG via Multi-Agent Semantic Rewriting
description: Multi-agent framework that sanitizes retrieved RAG context through semantic rewriting, achieving confidentiality without sacrificing factual or contextual fidelity.

title: Grading the Grader: Lessons from Evaluating an Agentic Data Analysis System
description: Evaluates agentic data-analysis systems by separating genuine output disagreements from grader noise, providing lessons for benchmarking complex code-and-text agents.

title: GroundEval: A Deterministic Replacement for LLM-as-Judge in Stateful Agent Evaluation
description: Evaluates stateful agents by deterministically checking what evidence they searched, fetched, cited, and wrote, removing stochastic bias from LLM-as-judge protocols.

title: EnterpriseClawBench: Benchmarking Agents from Real Workplace Sessions
description: Agent benchmark built from proprietary enterprise sessions, measuring how well agents handle heterogeneous files, tool calls, and real business-artifact generation.

title: Self-Compacting Language Model Agents
description: Proposes trajectory-aware compaction for long-lived agents, replacing fixed-interval token-threshold compaction with content-sensitive strategies that reduce context bloat.

title: Ponytrail: A local audit trail for AI coding-agent edits
description: Lightweight audit trail that records and surfaces every edit an AI coding agent makes, giving developers transparent visibility before changes reach version control.

title: PMB: Local-first persistent memory for AI coding agents over MCP
description: Open-source MCP-based memory layer that gives Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex a local SQLite-backed memory for decisions, lessons, and facts — offline and multilingual.

title: PMB: Local-first persistent memory for AI coding agents over MCP
description: Open-source MCP-based memory layer that gives Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex a local SQLite-backed memory for decisions, lessons, and facts — offline and multilingual.

title: Agent-as-a-Router: Agentic Model Routing for Coding Tasks
description: Treats model routing as a Context→Action→Feedback→Context loop that accumulates execution-grounded experience, achieving lower cumulative regret than static routers on coding benchmarks.

title: How a Poisoned Coding Test Turned an AI Agent Into an Attacker
description: Security research showing how a tampered take-home coding assessment can trick AI coding agents into exfiltrating cloud credentials and executing destructive actions. Updated June 22, 2026 with attack chain details and mitigation guidance.

title: Introducing eve — Vercel's Open-Source Agent Framework
description: Vercel open-sources eve, a filesystem-first agent framework built on the same infrastructure powering their 100+ production agents. Ships with durable execution, sandboxed compute, subagents, human-in-the-loop approvals, OpenTelemetry tracing, MCP/OpenAPI connectors, and a built-in eval system out of the box.

title: OpenAI Codex OSS Mode: Run Codex with Any Open-Source Model
description: OpenAI adds an OSS mode to Codex CLI allowing the App, CLI, and SDK to connect to any local or open-source model provider via Ollama, LM Studio, MLX, or vLLM instead of exclusively cloud models. Enables running GPT-OSS-20B and GPT-OSS-120B open-weight models natively in the Codex workflow.

title: Prompt Injection as Role Confusion
description: Research revealing that current LLMs prioritize text style over explicit role tags, making reliable role distinction impossible and enabling jailbreaks. Destyling text reduces prompt injection success from 61% to 10%.

title: The AI Governance Checklist That Maps to Your Stack — OpenRouter Blog
description: Argues that governance checklists must map directly to LLM routing architecture, showing how managed versus self-hosted gateways provide different default audit, spend, and data sovereignty capabilities.

title: Enforce AI Data Residency at the Routing Layer — OpenRouter Blog
description: Demonstrates how API teams can enforce geographic data residency via routing constraints instead of building local infrastructure, with concrete OpenRouter provider-order and Zero Data Retention examples.

title: AI News Today June 22 2026: 15 Biggest Stories
description: Roundup of major AI stories including the Fable 5 free trial expiration, Agentjacking attacks hitting 2,388 orgs, GLM-5.2 benchmark results at 1/6th GPT-5.5 cost, and GPT-5.6 previewed for late June.

title: From Fragments to Paths: Task-Level Context Recovery for Large Industrial Codebases
description: Introduces DeepDiscovery, a two-stage Location–Inference pipeline that recovers task-relevant context across large repositories without offline preprocessing, achieving a 78.6% SWE-bench Verified solve rate and 8.2 pp gains over baselines.

title: Autonomous Long-Running Coding Agents
description: Deep dive into loop engineering for autonomous coding agents, covering goals, evaluators, verifiers, and artifact-driven workflows that let agents continue working without human input. Covers Claude Code goal/loop modes and production harness patterns.

title: Prompt Engineering Is Dying: Loop Engineering Is Next
description: Loop engineering is emerging as the higher-leverage discipline behind reliable AI agent workflows. Covers goal definition, context curation, verification design, safe stopping, and bounded workflows across Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor.

title: The 7 Types of Agent Memory: A Technical Guide for AI Engineers
description: Covers working, semantic, episodic, procedural, retrieval, parametric, and prospective memory for AI agents, with use-case mapping and a Python implementation showing how each memory type fits into a production agent stack.

title: Daily AI Agent News Roundup — June 21, 2026
description: Curated roundup on the shift from model optimization to harness engineering for AI agents. Covers production failure analysis, observability design, control loops, agent-loop architecture, and why the harness is now the bottleneck.

title: AI Builders Digest — Sunday, June 21, 2026
description: Curated digest covering Vercel CEO on Markdown as the new programming language for AI agents, Box CEO on context handoff failures, Claude Code used to decode Linear A, and the rising hybrid skill of developer + agent systems.

title: Best Local Model for Vision — 2nd Benchmark Update — 21 Jun 2026
description: Community benchmark update evaluating local vision models on Raspberry Pi / edge hardware. Covers inference speed, quality vs. size tradeoffs, and practical local-deployment findings for RPi and consumer GPUs.

title: Temporary Cloudflare Accounts for AI Agents
description: Cloudflare now lets AI agents deploy Workers without a pre-created account via temporary deployments. Includes a live test using GPT-5.5 in Codex Desktop to build and claim an ephemeral project, observed and reported by Simon Willison.

title: Paving the Way for Agents in Biology
description: Anthropic research highlighting that biological data infrastructure was not designed for AI agents, creating critical friction for scientific automation. The VirBench benchmark tests 120 viral sequence retrieval queries and reveals substantial gaps between current agent capabilities and reproducible biological data access patterns.

title: GitHub Copilot Now Supports Claude Fable 5 Integration Despite Global Ban
description: OpenRouter blog covering developer tooling landscape in June 2026, including the fallout from Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 global suspension, GitHub Copilot's integration choices, the shift from prompt engineering to loop engineering for agent reliability, and the rising importance of harness design over model selection.

title: OpenCode Changelog: v1.17.9 with MCP Fixes and GLM-5.2 Support
description: Latest OpenCode updates include MCP OAuth fixes, Devstral model detection, new high/max thinking variants for GLM-5.2, improved timeout handling for long-running MCP tools, and step limit enforcement to prevent mid-run failures.

title: AI News Today - June 21, 2026: 16 Biggest Stories
description: Comprehensive roundup covering Fable 5 ban developments (Day 9), Anthropic's Seoul office opening with major Korean enterprise adoption (NAVER, Samsung SDS, LG CNS), and the White House's zero-jailbreak precondition for model restoration.

title: Beyond Simpson's Paradox: A Cascade of Confounders in AI Agent Pull-Request Co-Authorship
description: Analyzes 33,596 PRs across Copilot, Devin, Cursor, Claude Code, and Codex, showing that co-authorship merge-rate effects dissolve once repository selection and PR structure are controlled, warning against reporting pooled agent statistics without stratification.

title: AI News Today - June 20, 2026: 16 Biggest Stories
description: Top stories include Noam Shazeer's departure from Google to OpenAI as Lead Architecture Researcher, Fable 5 refund deadline passing, China's $295B five-year AI infrastructure plan, and FERC's historic grid interconnection orders for AI data centers.

title: Awesome Harness Engineering: Tools, Patterns, and Evals for AI Agents
description: Curated list for AI agent harness engineering covering tools, patterns, evaluations, memory, MCP, permissions, observability, and orchestration. Updated June 20, 2026 with new Life-Harness additions to the Agent Loop section.

title: The Router Era: Why Not Owning a Frontier Model Became an Advantage
description: Analysis of how model routing has become a competitive moat in mid-2026, with no single model dominating all tasks. Covers OpenRouter, Factory Router, and how companies like Cursor, OpenCode, and Cognition Devin treat model-agnosticism as a feature.

title: Connect OpenClaw to OpenRouter: One Key, Failover, Free Models
description: Step-by-step integration that binds OpenClaw to OpenRouter’s unified API, enabling automatic failover, access to free models, and a single credential for multi-model workflows.

title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
description: New inference-time method for tool-calling agents that maintains observed task states in a separate ledger, rendering them into prompts and validating state-dependent policy constraints before execution. Improves average pass^k across customer-service domains.

title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
description: New benchmark evaluating 24 LLMs across 12 programming languages (extending LiveCodeBench beyond Python). Reveals Python overfitting, language-specific contamination, and substantial multilingual performance disparities in current models.

title: Automating SKILL.md Generation for Computer-Using Agents via Interaction Trajectory Mining
description: 3-stage pipeline to automatically generate SKILL.md documentation for computer-using agents by segmenting GUI trajectories, clustering into candidate skills, and training skill-aware policies. Diagnostic study revealing current limitations in cross-domain transfer.

title: Agentic Coding and Persistent Returns to Expertise
description: Anthropic analyzes ~400,000 Claude Code sessions: users make ~70% of planning decisions while Claude handles ~80% of execution. Domain expertise (not coding proficiency) is the strongest success predictor — expert users see 2x higher verified success rates. Debugging time fell by half across the 7-month study window.

title: Google and Kaggle's GenAI Intensive Vibe Coding Course 2026
description: Free 5-day AI Agents Intensive course ran June 15–19, 2026, covering production-ready agent development with natural language as the primary interface, connecting external tools and APIs, and a hands-on capstone project. Course refreshed with new expert speakers following the popular 2025 inaugural run.

title: LLM-as-Code: Agentic Programming for Agent Harness
description: Accepted to KDD 2026 Workshop on Agentic Software Engineering. Proposes 'Agentic Programming' — the host program retains full control flow, and the LLM is invoked as an adaptive component only for reasoning/generation steps. Addresses token explosion, control-flow hallucination, and unreliable completion as architectural bugs in current orchestrator-only agent designs.

title: Agentic Software: How AI Agents Are Restructuring the Software Paradigm
description: arXiv paper formalizing 'Agentic Engineering' as a distinct discipline from traditional software engineering. Argues that the LLM is the software (not code), with three core shifts: Agentic Engineering as a new object of study, LLM-driven control models replacing human-predefined logic, and the human role shifting to 'intent architect'. Includes a 4-stage roadmap for self-evolving agent ecosystems.
